evidence_quotes.quote: problematic commercial practices of social media influencers
video games being destroyed by publishers after we have purchased the games

paragraphs.text: As an EU citizen, I am writing this feedback to express my support for The Digital Fairness Act with its mission to protect consumers and effective functioning of the Digital Single Market. For far too long, corporations and businesses have ployed different (psychological and unfair) tactics to take advantage of consumers in the online space in order to line their pockets. Their greed knows no bound and if they are not made to reel themselves in, I, as many others, fear that they will continue to find ways to further exploit consumers. I am glad to see that the EU commission is coming forward with means to try and regulate these corporations/businesses on their use of dark patterns, addictive designs, personalised targeting, difficulties managing digital subscriptions, and problematic commercial practices of social media influencers. A provision I believe that could be added to the list is one, where consumers are provided with protections from our video games being destroyed by publishers after we have purchased the games (e.g. The Crew or Overwatch). There are hundreds of games that have been destroyed already and hundreds more are at risk of being destroyed. Because of this an EU citizens' initiative was started to 'Stop killing Games' and has surpassed the one million threshold needed to be valid. I hope the Digital Fairness Act is able to materialize so that it can enforce changes in favor of consumers in the online space.
